items.title: SA government apologises to prisoner denied abortion medication

children.content: The South Australian ombudsman calls for changes to medical monitoring at the Adelaide Women's Prison after a female prisoner was denied a medical abortion, which the health minister says is unacceptable.

gallery.altText: A building surrounded by a fence and trees

thumbnail.altText: A building surrounded by a fence and trees

items.title: Players 'have not heard' rumours Saudi PIF may cut LIV Golf funding

children.content: Major champion Sergio Garcia says LIV Golf players "have not heard anything" as media speculation about the future financing of the breakaway league intensifies.

image.altText: Golfer on the tee, looking at the ball after his drive, holding the club across his chest

items.title: Teen admits creating deepfakes in Australian-first prosecution

children.content: An Adelaide teenager who was the first person charged under new nationwide laws aimed at tackling adult deepfake pornography has pleaded guilty to two counts of creating or altering sexual material without consent.

image.altText: William Yeates is wearing a white shirt and looking over his shoulder

items.title: Power confirms appeal after reasons released for Butters AFL Tribunal decision

children.content: The AFL Tribunal releases its reasons for finding Zak Butters guilty of abusive and insulting language to umpire Nick Foot, saying it was "satisfied to the requisite standard" that he had made the remarks and that it was "implausible" that the umpire would have made it up.

articles.title: Player's umpire abuse verdict raises questions about AFL's 'conflict of interest'

children.content: The reporting of Port Adelaide player Zak Butters for umpire abuse sparks debate about whether officials should be involved with betting and if better audio recordings should be available from games.

image.altText: A Port Adelaide AFL player (middle) stands in the rain staring up field with a concerned look on his face.

articles.title: Butters asked 'why did you pay that?' Koch says

children.content: Port Adelaide chairman David Koch says the AFL club will back Zak Butters "to the hilt" amid dispute over what was said to an umpire during Sunday night's loss against St Kilda.

image.altText: AFL player Zak Butters at a media conference.

gallery.altText: A Port Adelaide AFL player (middle) stands in the rain staring up field with a concerned look on his face.

thumbnail.altText: A Port Adelaide AFL player (middle) stands in the rain staring up field with a concerned look on his face.

items.title: 'Calculated' theft of $12,000 worth of fuel prompts arrest

children.content: A 36-year-old man has been charged with theft amid an investigation into as many as 20 petrol drive-offs, which police say involved an SUV that contained a tank used to carry stolen diesel.

image.altText: The back of a car with its boot open.

items.title: The outback jobs disappearing as fuel costs hit tourism

children.content: From roadhouses to stations, outback workers are losing their jobs as tourists stay away. Skyrocketing fuel prices, as well as concerns around the availability of fuel in remote locations, are causing a 76 per cent loss of revenue for tourist-dependent businesses.

image.altText: A young woman bottle feeds a calf and a lamb.

items.title: Man says he was acting in 'self-defence' when he kicked a dog twice in the head

children.content: A northern suburbs man says he had been bitten and was acting in self-defence when he kicked a dog that was tethered to the tray of a ute in a Bunnings car park.

image.altText: Maya was allegedly kicked to the head while in a Bunnings car park

items.title: 'Fierce and defiant' disability advocate remembered after drowning

children.content: Ellen Fraser-Barbour is being remembered for her tireless advocacy and love of nature after her death at the age of 35.

image.altText: A disability advocate with her friend.

items.title: Blow to cartography in Australia as The Map Shop prepares to close

children.content: One of Australia's few remaining cartography shops is about to close in Adelaide, prompting questions about where people can buy the critical documents, particularly those travelling in remote areas where mobile coverage is limited.

gallery.altText: Man stands in front of shelf of maps

thumbnail.altText: Man stands in front of shelf of maps

items.title: 'She was extraordinary': Famed sculptor Pat Moseley remembered

children.content: Across dusty outback tracks to the promenades of Adelaide, sculptor Pat Moseley turned limestone and bronze into the faces of legends.

image.altText: black and white photo of artist with horse sculptors

items.title: Billionaire's 'slow moving car crash' leads to individuals being investigated by ASIC

children.content: After years of financial red flags, corporate watchdog ASIC confirms it has multiple investigations underway into Sanjeev Gupta's troubled empire, following multiple collapses that have left hundreds of workers facing an uncertain future.

image.altText: GFG Alliance executive chair Sanjeev Gupta.

items.title: Residents near cement plant raise concerns about more plastic being burnt for fuel

children.content: Residents living near a large cement plant in Adelaide are concerned about the owner wanting to burn more plastic as fuel to replace natural gas.

image.altText: aerial image of a manufacturing plant with river to the right and cloudy sky in background

items.title: Ukraine looking to 'capitalise' on new momentum against Russia

children.content: Western officials and analysts say Ukrainian forces have disrupted Russia's planned spring offensive and managed to retake territory.

image.altText: Soldier stands in a field launching a fixed wing drone.

items.title: Why Australia turned to tiny nation to secure critical fertiliser supplies

children.content: The Middle East conflict has disrupted the flow of Australia's imports of the fertiliser urea. Facing the potential threat of reduced crop yields, Anthony Albanese looked to Australia's neighbours.

articles.title: Albanese to meet Malaysian PM on last day of fuel campaign tour

children.content: The PM will have a one-on-one meeting with Malaysian Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim in Kuala Lumpur and talks with a global oil giant on the last day of his south-east Asian tour. Follow live.

image.altText: Albanese presser

gallery.altText: Anthony Albanese in a grey and green jacket and white hard hat, smiling while holding up two jars of blue and brown urea.

thumbnail.altText: Anthony Albanese in a grey and green jacket and white hard hat, smiling while holding up two jars of blue and brown urea.

items.title: Albanese government's frustration rises as Australians pay price for war

children.content: Fuel prices and inflation aren't the only metrics climbing as the Strait of Hormuz remains shut. The Albanese government's level of frustration is rising just as fast.

image.altText: Anthony Albanese in a jacket and tie

avatar.alt: David Speers sits on a couch in front of a yellow screen, smiling at the camera, in a suit and open-necked shirt.

items.title: Markets set to open flat with fuel crisis likely to worsen

children.content: The fuel crisis is set to worsen after a massive fire at one of Australia's two oil refineries. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: ASX

items.title: Gina Rinehart 'won' the legal battle for Hope Downs, commentator says

children.content: Gina Rinehart and her company, Hancock Prospecting, are the "clear winners" of what prominent Perth industry commentator Tim Treadgold said was the "big daddy" of legal battles.

image.altText: A woman sits next to a man at a conference.

items.title: WA Police won't remove racist comments on its social media posts

children.content: Racist comments are being published on WA Police social media posts with little moderation, but the force says it is not prepared to switch off comments.

image.altText: A photo over someone's shoulder of them holding a phone with the WA Police Force facebook page on it.

items.title: Kerr scores landmark goal as Matildas win FIFA Series

children.content: Sam Kerr's 75th international goal has helped the Matildas to a surprisingly tough, controversial 2-0 victory over Kenya in the FIFA Series final.

gallery.altText: Female football players on the podium, captain in centre lifting trophy

thumbnail.altText: Female football players on the podium, captain in centre lifting trophy

items.title: Supermarkets stalling over pressure to lift milk prices

children.content: Supermarket milk price rises linked to the war in Iran are predicted in the coming months as hundreds of dairy farmers battling increasing costs receive more for their product.

image.altText: A line of cows walk into one side of the bales to get ready to be milked.

items.title: Ticketmaster and Live Nation hold 'harmful' anticompetitive monopoly, US jury finds

children.content: A New York jury finds concert giant Live Nation and its Ticketmaster subsidiary have a harmful monopoly over big concert venues.

image.altText: The word ticketmaster is seen in white on a blue background, on an advert along the side of a sports field.

items.title: Back Roads: How this tiny town welcomed a 70-piece orchestra

children.content: The Sydney Youth Orchestras travelled to this tiny town to help inspire the local kids, but when they left, they were the ones who felt changed.

image.altText: An orchestra performs on stage.

items.title: Rebel Wilson's directorial debut hits Aus cinemas amid legal dispute

children.content: Plus a powerful look Palestinian life through multiple generations and a buzzy Japanese video game adaptation makes it to Australian shores.

image.altText: Three women stand in front of a stage with blue and red velvet curtains

items.title: Quiz: Can you tell the difference between The Pitt and E.R.?

children.content: Ahead of The Pitt season finale dropping this week, can you pick the difference between it and its hospital drama ancestor E.R.?

image.altText: Composite image of Noah Wyle as a young and older doctor

items.title: Major upgrades planned for 200-hectare site once used as TV, film backdrop

children.content: Australian-based global tour operator Intrepid has announced a multi-million-dollar investment in Central Australia's Ooraminna Homestead, 30 kilometres south of Alice Springs.

gallery.altText: A wide angle shot of a small tin cabin nestled among trees in a rocky outback landscape.

thumbnail.altText: A wide angle shot of a small tin cabin nestled among trees in a rocky outback landscape.

items.title: Containers for Change set to include wine bottles, but industry unhappy

children.content: A newly formed West Australian wine group warns that bottles of wine produced in the state could increase by $1.25 if the expansion of the Containers for Change scheme goes ahead.

image.altText: A pile of glass bottles

items.title: Proposal to block non-citizens from first homebuyer scheme slammed

children.content: Multicultural groups are concerned Angus Taylor's proposal to prevent permanent residents from accessing the Australian Government 5% Deposit Scheme could deter skilled migrants from coming to Australia.

image.altText: Mr taylor, in suit and tie, speaks to the media in a passage at Parliament House.

items.title: Woman suffers brain injury after 'toxic dose' of wrong drug

children.content: A 36-year-old woman who says she suffered a "hypoxic brain injury" when she was given a "toxic dose" of the wrong drug during routine surgery at Palmerston Regional Hospital is suing the Northern Territory government for negligence.

image.altText: A patient laying on a hospital bed, knees up with blanket covering their legs, holding a cup next to urine sample container

items.title: Arrest warrant issued in legal battle for $27k in unpaid long service leave

children.content: It's now been over three years since two women left their jobs at a small business. Two courts later, they want their combined $27,000 in long service leave paid out to them, and say they won't give up until they get it.

image.altText: two women sitting at a dining table looking through paperwork

items.title: 'Canary in coal mine' warning as marine pest found in new area

children.content: The discovery on Tasmania's west coast has scientists and fishers alike fearing the spread of a destructive species to another previously untouched part of the state's marine environment.

items.title: A lucky chat, then a lifeline for men with prostate cancer

children.content: Michael Platts panicked when he was diagnosed with prostate cancer. Now he wants to help others break through the stigma and silence surrounding the condition.

image.altText: A farmer in his 60s in a blue shirt stands in a paddock looking into the camera.

items.title: Manhunt underway after prisoner mistakenly released from jail

children.content: The ABC understands Kyle Quayle was wrongfully released from Clarence Correctional Centre on Tuesday and is believed to be in the Newcastle area.

image.altText: head shot of a man with medium length brown hair, orange clothes

items.title: Adam was quoted $60,000 for his home insurance. His local council is taking action

children.content: Adam Osborne was quoted a staggering $60,000 for his home insurance this year. With others in the same boat, six Queensland councils are looking at creating their own insurance mutual to drive down premiums.

image.altText: man in blue workshirt walking along the river

items.title: Henderson 'content' knowing her epic reign will not last forever

children.content: Rebecca Henderson has won 13 elite national mountain bike cross-country titles in a row and, this season, is unbeaten in the UCI Oceania Continental series, which concludes this weekend at the Cairns Rainforest Rumble.

items.title: Short-stay levy overperforms, as holiday rental growth stagnates in Victoria

children.content: Data suggests the growth in short-stay accommodation listings in Victoria has slowed in the year after the Victorian government's new tax came into effect.
